threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for your coffee with three shots and a spritz of vanilla; everyone else just orders a flat white and moves on.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week. A group of teens were dissecting conspiracy theories about the Tubes. Heard them swear the Underground's haunted.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw an old man feeding pigeons by the canal while a group of hipsters set up for an outdoor yoga class nearby.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find late-night techno trains. Just hop on a U-Bahn and you might ride straight to a rave—disco lights included.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: When someone knows to avoid BART during a smoke scare at Civic Center, they're a local. Tourists just look confused on their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a tourist ask the driver if the Muni was free. The driver just chuckled and pointed at the fare box. Priceless look on their face!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but they miss the quiet charm of our plaça corners where locals escape the crowds for a copa.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still marvel at the street performers in Plaça del Sol, while the locals just walk by, unfazed.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when the bus driver shouts at pedestrians. It’s just a friendly London greeting, trust me.
Last night on the 59, a bloke in a bright orange tracksuit serenaded us with a questionable rendition of 'Wonderwall' for five stops straight.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was living here when I saw a pack of kids turn a bus stop into a makeshift soccer field while waiting for the 104. No one batted an eye.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst from a street stall while listening to a spontaneous jam session. It's just another Tuesday here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know the shortcuts through alleys to avoid the BART delays. The tourists still clutch their maps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a saxophonist jamming out at the Powell station while a gaggle of tourists snapped pics—Muni moments like that remind you why we love this chaotic city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every tapas bar is the same, but the secret to Gràcia's charm is in the little neighborhoods ones you can barely find.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they struggle to navigate the free metro map, still not used to the city's quirks.
